_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Identify optimal locations for network sensor placement to collect on targeted devices.
reference_document_element_description: Skill in building dashboard layouts that communicate KPIs clearly, prioritize decision-relevant information, and scale across device types and user roles.
comments: DCWF task intersects with WIDAI AB domain. AB-S-006 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: TF-K-013 (TF, cosine: 0.286), DA-T-009 (DA, cosine: 0.263).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 2.5/10 (raw 0.250). No substantive shared vocabulary — relationship is purely semantic. DCWF-only terms [collect, devices, identify, locations, network, optimal] vs WIDAI-only terms [across, building, clearly, communicate, dashboard, decision] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.864 (P=0.873, R=0.855). Balanced P/R — the 12-word DCWF element and 20-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: contradiction-dominant (88.9%). Despite 'Intersects with' classification, the model detects semantic tension between "Identify optimal locations for network sensor placement to collect on targeted d" and "Skill in building dashboard layouts that communicate KPIs clearly, prioritize de". Indicates these concepts operate in adjacent but distinct professional domains — shared vocabulary, different application context.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.325 > STS 0.250 (gap +0.074). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [none] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 0.0%. Zero substantive shared tokens. The entire relationship is semantic — no lexical trace. DCWF terms: [collect, devices, identify, locations, network, optimal]. WIDAI terms: [across, building, clearly, communicate, dashboard, decision].
